Document:
# One-Handed Melee
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/One-Handed%20Melee"/]
## One-Handed Melee
One-handed melee weapons are fast, easy to wield, and leave your other hand free for a shield, tool, or secondary weapon. They are ideal for close-quarters combat and self-defense.
The most basic of survival tools - useful for crafting, hunting, and fighting.
[bold]Weapon[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] [bold]Combat Knife[/bold] Military-grade knife. Balanced for combat. [bold]Kitchen Knife[/bold] Common utility blade. Found in settlements. [bold]Tribal Knife[/bold] Handcrafted. Slightly shorter but reliable. [bold]Butcher's Cleaver[/bold] Heavy cutting blade. Good damage for a one-handed weapon.
Fast and reliable. A staple tool and weapon throughout the Wasteland.
[bold]Weapon[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] [bold]Hatchet[/bold] Standard cutting axe. Common tool and weapon. [bold]Tribal Hatchet[/bold] Handcrafted variant. Slightly rougher than a manufactured hatchet. [bold]Fire Axe[/bold] Large head, heavy swing. Deals significant damage.
Non-lethal options for subduing enemies, or improvised weapons when nothing else is available.
[bold]Weapon[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] [bold]Police Baton[/bold] Pre-war law enforcement baton. Good for non-lethal takedowns. [bold]Tribal Club[/bold] Simple blunt weapon. Easy to find or make. [bold]Broken Pipe[/bold] Improvised weapon. Scavenged from ruins.
One-handed blades that deal more damage than knives while remaining swift.
[bold]Weapon[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] [bold]Machete[/bold] Heavy cutting blade. Good against lightly armored targets. [bold]Scrap Machete[/bold] Post-war constructed machete. Functional but rougher. [bold]Chinese Sword[/bold] Pre-war officer's blade. Well-balanced and durable. [bold]Ceremonial Sword[/bold] Decorative but functional. Often carried by Legion officers.
